shoot_guide.title: SHOOT GUIDE
shoot_guide.subtitle: BoomBoom Nasal Stick
shoot_guide.product_summary: BoomBoom Nasal Stick — 5 Heroes, 5 Remixes (3 VO + 2 No-Voice)
shoot_guide.tagline: 10 videos from one shoot — here's everything you need to capture

heroes.title: The Blind Sniff Test
notes: She does NOT know what scent she's about to try. Cover the label or have someone hand it to her.
Camera tight on her face for the reaction. This is the money shot — don't cut away.
Let her react for a full 3 seconds before she says anything. The face sells it.
Product label revealed AFTER the reaction — not before.

lines.text: "My friend just handed me this and said 'breathe in' and won't tell me what it is."
[looks at the stick suspiciously, sniffs the outside]
"OK… here we go."
[takes a deep hit — eyes go wide, jaw drops, grabs the counter]
"WHAT. What is— oh my God. It's like someone pressure-washed the inside of my head."
[turns the stick around to read the label for the first time]
"BoomBoom? This little thing? It's just essential oils? Bro. I need like ten of these."
"Link. Now. Go."

heroes.title: The Allergy Season Panic
notes: She looks genuinely congested or tired at the start. Real 'I can't breathe' energy.
Urgency is the driver — everyone she knows is stuffed up, she was too, until this.
Show the product as her survival tool, not a nice-to-have.
Keep the energy slightly panicked at the top, then relieved after the hit.

lines.text: "If your allergies are destroying you right now, stop scrolling."
"I literally could not breathe through my nose for three days. Three. Days."
"My friend gave me this little stick and said just try it."
[takes a hit — immediate visible relief, deep breath through nose]
"I can BREATHE. Like right now. That was instant. It's menthol and eucalyptus and peppermint."
"I bought six of these. I'm not going through that again. Link's right here — go before allergy season gets worse."

heroes.title: The Stuffed vs. Clear
notes: This is a before/after video. The transformation IS the content.
Start with her visibly congested — mouth breathing, puffy, tired eyes.
Time the 'after' moment on camera. Don't cut. Let the viewer watch it happen live.
Split-screen energy even if it's not literally split screen. The contrast has to be obvious.

lines.text: "Watch what happens to my face in the next ten seconds."
[visibly congested — breathing through mouth, looks miserable]
[picks up BoomBoom stick, takes one long hit through each nostril]
[holds breath for a beat — then exhales slowly through nose, eyes close in relief]
"There it is. That's the face of someone who can breathe again."
"It's just a little essential oil stick. Menthol, eucalyptus, peppermint. One breath and you're back."
"Ten million sold. Shark Tank. I'll link it."

heroes.title: The Gym Pre-Workout Hack
notes: Gym setting or workout clothes visible. She's about to work out.
The problem is specific: can't breathe properly during cardio and heavy lifts.
Product reveal at the solution moment — not before.
Keep it fast. Problem → solution → proof → link.

lines.text: "The pre-workout hack nobody talks about."
"I kept getting gassed out on cardio because I couldn't breathe through my nose. Like my lungs were fine but my nose was just… shut."
"Then someone at my gym showed me this."
[pulls BoomBoom from gym bag, takes a hit]
"Instantly. Like everything just opened. It's menthol and eucalyptus — you just breathe in right before your set."
"It's like five bucks. I'm linking it."

heroes.title: The Mom Who Tried Everything
notes: This is a stitch/reply energy video — she's responding to the hype around this product.
Start skeptical. She saw it everywhere, finally tried it, now she gets it.
The social proof is the driver: millions sold, Shark Tank, everyone on TikTok talking about it.
Her personal reaction validates what everyone else is saying.

lines.text: "OK I finally tried the thing that everyone on TikTok won't shut up about."
"I kept seeing this little nasal stick everywhere. Ten million sold. Was on Shark Tank. And I was like… it can't be that serious."
[takes a hit]
"Oh. Oh no. OK I get it now."
"It's like someone turned on the AC inside my nose. I can feel it in my whole face."
"Yeah. I'm linking this. I already ordered three more. I hate when TikTok is right."

broll.description: Product tube close-up on flat surface, brand name and cap visible. Clean background.
Uncapping the stick. Slow, satisfying cap removal. Close on hands.
Stick held up to nose, side angle. Deep inhale. Close on face — capture the exact moment.
Multiple scents laid out on flat surface — variety pack if she has more than one.
Back of packaging or label — ingredients visible (menthol, eucalyptus, peppermint).
Size comparison — stick next to lipstick, pen, or keys to show how small it is.
Rolling the stick between fingers. Texture and portability moment.
Dropping the stick into gym bag or purse. Quick, casual.
Pulling it from pocket mid-conversation — like she always has it on her.
Stick on nightstand next to phone/alarm clock. Morning light context.
Before/after face: first shot mouth-breathing and miserable, second shot deep nasal inhale with eyes closed.
Post-workout context — catching her breath, reaches for stick from gym bag.
The shock face — eyes wide, jaw drops, hand goes to mouth after first hit.
The relief exhale — long slow breath out through nose, visible relaxation in face.
Handing the stick to someone else — their first reaction too.

voiceovers.script: OK so everyone kept telling me about this little nasal stick. Like everyone. TikTok, my gym, my friend group. Finally tried it. You just breathe in and it's like someone flipped a switch in your sinuses. Menthol, eucalyptus, peppermint — all natural. They sold over ten million of these. It was on Shark Tank. And honestly? I get the hype now. I keep one in my bag and one on my nightstand. Link's right here.

Allergy season has been absolutely wrecking me this year. Like I could not breathe through my nose for days. And then someone handed me this little essential oil stick called BoomBoom. One breath and everything just… opened. Instant. I bought a six pack because I'm not going back to mouth breathing. If your sinuses are fighting you right now — this. Link below.

Things that actually changed my gym sessions. This little nasal stick. You breathe in right before a set and your airways just open up completely. Menthol, eucalyptus, peppermint. It costs like five dollars and it lasts a month. I used to get gassed out because I couldn't breathe through my nose. Not anymore. I'll link it.
